text:
Brent crude is now a pre-war levels at around $72.5 a barrel. 

The Brent futures market peaked on April 30th at $126.41 a barrel.

Oil-rich Venezuela was hit by one of the biggest earthquakes in its history (two consecutive tremors) on Wednesday evening, toppling buildings in Caracas. Early reports say oil production and exports appear unaffected. 

 https://t.co/N4zx5ojg5P

Iraq to OPEC: increase our oil production quota — or else!!!

https://t.co/eLDsy0pM0d

Saudi Arabia appears to be about re-starting oil loadings inside the Persian Gulf. 

Three Saudi-owned in ballast VLCCs are concentrating around the Ju’aymah terminal (it has 6 single point moorings).

If the loadings go ahead, it would be the first known since early March.

The three tankers crossed the Strait of Hormuz into the Persian Gulf over the last 48 hours with their location transponder off. Other Saudi owned VLCCs that were just outside the SoH appear to have gone “dark” too, indicating more potential inbound tankers.

Beijing’s oil import strike continues: 

Chinese companies sold West Africa crude on Thursday, with several Nigerian and Angolan grades hitting the widest discount in at least ~15 years to the Dated Brent benchmark. 

If China doesn’t show up very, very soon (or Iran doesn’t up its Hormuz games), it’s going to get ugly in the physical oil market.
IMO pauses ship evacuation plan from inside the Strait of Hormuz after an attack of a vessel near the coast of Oman https://t.co/HZBsZ5WsLq
